KEY

Cellulosome
dockerin
Cellulosome - Dockerin Module - major building blocks of scaffoldins of cellulosomes
cohesin
Cellulosome - Cohesin Module - anchor the catalytic enzymes to the scaffoldin of cellulosomes
SLH
Cellulosome - S-Layer Homology Module - anchoring module of type II cellulosomes

Glycoside Hydrolases
GH1

GlycosylTransferases - GT31 - N-acetyllactosaminide β-1,3-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.149); Glycoprotein-N-acetylgalactosamine 3-β-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.122); fucose-specific β-1,3-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); globotriosylceramide β-1,3-GalNAc transferase (EC 2.4.1.79); chondroitin synthase (β-1,3-GlcUA and β-1,4-GalNAc transferase (EC 2.4.1.175); chondroitin β-1,3-glucuronyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.226); chondroitin β-1,4-N-acetylgalactosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Gal: β-galactosylxylosylprotein β-1,3-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.134); UDP-GlcNAc: O-fucosylpeptide β-1,3-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.222)
GT32
GlycosylTransferases - GT32 - α-1,6-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); α-1,4-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); α-1,4-N-acetylgalactosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); GDP-Man: inositol-phosphorylceramide transferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Gal: β-galactoside α-1,4-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Gal: lactose/N-acetyl-lactosamine α-1,4-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Glc: protein α-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT33
GlycosylTransferases - GT33 - GDP-Man: chitobiosyldiphosphodolichol β-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.142).
GT34
GlycosylTransferases - GT34 - UDP-Gal: galactomannan α-1,6-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Xyl: xyloglucan α-1,6-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.39); α-1,2-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT35
GlycosylTransferases - GT35 - glycogen or starch phosphorylase (EC 2.4.1.1).
GT37
GlycosylTransferases - GT37 - galactoside 2-L-fucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.69)
GT38
GlycosylTransferases - GT38 - polysialyltransferase (EC 2.4.-.-)
GT39
GlycosylTransferases - GT39 - Dol-P-Man: protein α-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.109)
GT40
GlycosylTransferases - GT40 - β-1,3-galactofuranosyltransferases (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT41
GlycosylTransferases - GT41 - UDP-GlcNAc: peptide β-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.255); UDP-Glc: peptide N-β-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); GDP-L-Fuc: protein O-α-L-fucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.221)
GT42
GlycosylTransferases - GT42 - CMP-NeuAc α-2,3-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.-)
GT43
GlycosylTransferases - GT43 - β-glucuronyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.135); UDP-Xyl: xylan β-1,4-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT44
GlycosylTransferases - GT44 - UDP-Glc: α-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-GlcNAc: α-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-).
GT45
GlycosylTransferases - GT45 - α-N-acteylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT47
GlycosylTransferases - GT47 - heparan β-glucuronyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.225); xyloglucan β-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); heparan synthase (EC 2.4.1.-); arabinan α-L-arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-).
GT48
GlycosylTransferases - GT48 - 1,3-β-glucan synthase (EC 2.4.1.34)
GT49
GlycosylTransferases - GT49 - β-1,3-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-).
GT50
GlycosylTransferases - GT50 - Dol-P-Man α-1,4-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT51
GlycosylTransferases - GT51 - murein polymerase (EC 2.4.1.129).
GT52
GlycosylTransferases - GT52 - α-2,3-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.4); α-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT53
GlycosylTransferases - GT53 - UDP-L-Ara: α-L-arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-); DecapreP-Araf: α-(1,3)-arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-); DecapreP-Araf: Lipoarabinomannan α-(1,5)-arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT54
GlycosylTransferases - GT54 - UDP-GlcNAc: α-1,3-D-mannoside β-1,4-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.145)
GT55
GlycosylTransferases - GT55 - GDP-Man: mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate synthase (EC 2.4.1.217)
GT56
GlycosylTransferases - GT56 - TDP-Fuc4NAc: lipid II Fuc4NAc transferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT57
GlycosylTransferases - GT57 - Dol-P-Glc: α-1,3-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT58
GlycosylTransferases - GT58 - Dol-P-Man: Man5GlcNAc2-PP-Dol α-1,3-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.258)
GT59
GlycosylTransferases - GT59 - Dol-P-Glc: Glc2Man9GlcNAc2-PP-Dol α-1,2-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.256)
GT60
GlycosylTransferases - GT60 - UDP-GlcNAc: polypeptide α-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-GlcNAc: hydroxyproline polypeptide α-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT61
GlycosylTransferases - GT61 - β-1,2-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.38) ; protein O-β-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.94) ; xylan α-1,3-arabinofuranosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-) ; UDP-GlcNAc: protein O-β-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.255); protein O-mannose -1,4-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.312)
GT62
GlycosylTransferases - GT62 - α-1,2-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); α-1,6-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT63
GlycosylTransferases - GT63 - UDP-Glc: DNA β-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.27)
GT64
GlycosylTransferases - GT64 - UDP-GlcNAc: heparan α-N-acetylhexosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.224)
GT65
GlycosylTransferases - GT65 - GDP-Fuc: protein O-α-fucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT66
GlycosylTransferases - GT66 - dolichyl-diphosphooligosaccharide protein glycotransferase (EC 2.4.99.18); undecaprenyl-diphosphooligosaccharide protein glycotransferase (EC 2.4.99.19)
GT67
GlycosylTransferases - GT67 - UDP-Gal: phosphoglycan β-1,3-galactosyltransferase 1 (SCG1) (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-GlcNAc β-1,2-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT68
GlycosylTransferases - GT68 - GDP-Fuc: protein O-α-fucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT69
GlycosylTransferases - GT69 - GDP-Man: α-1,3-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT70
GlycosylTransferases - GT70 - UDP-GlcA: β-glucuronosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.17)
GT71
GlycosylTransferases - GT71 - α-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT72
GlycosylTransferases - GT72 - UDP-Glc: DNA α-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.26)
GT73
GlycosylTransferases - GT73 - CMP-β-KDO: α-3-deoxy-D-manno-octulosonic-acid (KDO) transferase (EC 2.4.99.-).
GT74
GlycosylTransferases - GT74 - α-1,2-L-fucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.69)
GT75
GlycosylTransferases - GT75 - UDP-Glc: self-glucosylating β-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-L-arabinopyranose mutase (EC 5.4.99.-)
GT76
GlycosylTransferases - GT76 - Dol-P-Man: α-1,6-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT77
GlycosylTransferases - GT77 - α-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.39); α-1,3-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.37); arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-);
GT78
GlycosylTransferases - GT78 - GDP-Man: α-mannosyltransferase (mannosylglycerate synthase) (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT79
GlycosylTransferases - GT79 - GDP-D-Ara: phosphoglycan α-1,2-D-arabinopyranosyltransferase 1 (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT80
GlycosylTransferases - GT80 - β-galactoside α-2,6-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.1); β-galactoside α-2,3-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.4)
GT81
GlycosylTransferases - GT81 - NDP-Glc: glucosyl-3-phosphoglycerate synthase (EC 2.4.1.266); NDP-Man: mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate synthase (EC 2.4.1.217);
GT82
GlycosylTransferases - GT82 - UDP-GalNAc: β-1,4-N-acetylgalactosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT83
GlycosylTransferases - GT83 - undecaprenyl phosphate-α-L-Ara4N: 4-amino-4-deoxy-β-L-arabinosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.43); dodecaprenyl phosphate-β-galacturonic acid: lipopolysaccharide core α-galacturonosyl transferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT84
GlycosylTransferases - GT84 - cyclic β-1,2-glucan synthase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT85
GlycosylTransferases - GT85 - β-D-arabinofuranosyl monophosphoryldecaprenol: galactan α-D-arabinofuranosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT87
GlycosylTransferases - GT87 - polyprenol-P-Man: α-1,2-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT88
GlycosylTransferases - GT88 - UDP-Glc: α-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT89
GlycosylTransferases - GT89 - β-D-arabinofuranosyl-1-monophosphoryldecaprenol : arabinan β-1,2-arabinofuranosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT90
GlycosylTransferases - GT90 - UDP-Xyl: (mannosyl) glucuronoxylomannan/galactoxylomannan β-1,2-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-); UDP-Glc: protein O-β-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Xyl: protein O-β-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-)
GT91
GlycosylTransferases - GT91 - β-1,2-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT92
GlycosylTransferases - GT92 - UDP-Gal: N-glycan core α-1,6-fucoside β-1,4-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-); UDP-Gal: β-galactoside β-1,4-galactosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT93
GlycosylTransferases - GT93 - UDP-GluA : α-glucuronyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-) involved in GAG polymerization
GT94
GlycosylTransferases - GT94 - GDP-Man: GlcA-β-1,2-Man-α-1,3-Glc-β-1,4-Glc-α-1-PP-undecaprenol β-1,4-mannosyltransferase (2.4.1.251)
GT95
GlycosylTransferases - GT95 - UDP-β-L-Araf:hydroxyproline β-L-arabinofuranosyltransferase (EC 2.4.2.-);
GT96

GT97
GlycosylTransferases - GT97 - CMP-Neu5Ac:α-galactoside α-2,6-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.-); CMP-Neu5Ac:α-glucoside α-2,6-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.-);
GT98

GT99
GlycosylTransferases - GT99 - CMP-β-KDO 3-deoxy-β-D-manno-oct-2-ulosonic acid transferase (EC 2.4.99.-)
GT100
GlycosylTransferases - GT100 - α-sialyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.-)
GT101
GlycosylTransferases - GT101 - β-glucosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT102
GlycosylTransferases - GT102 - dTDP-β-L-Rhap : O-antigen-polysaccharide α-1,3-L-rhamnosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.289)
GT103
GlycosylTransferases - GT103 - UDP-α-L-GlcpNAc: O-antigen-polysaccharide β-1,4-N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.56)

GT106
GlycosylTransferases - GT106 - UDP-β-L-rhamnose:rhamnogalacturonan I 4-α-rhamnosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.351)
GT107

GlycosylTransferases - GT108 - GDP-α-D-Manp: β-1,2-D-mannosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.374)

GT110

GT111
GlycosylTransferases - GT111 - UDP-Galf: β-1,3-galactofuranosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)
GT112
GlycosylTransferases - GT112 - ADP-glyceromannoheptose: glycoprotein O-heptosyltransferase (EC 2.4.99.-)
GT113
GlycosylTransferases - GT113 - Glucosyltransferase (2.4.1.-); Hexosyltransferase (2.4.1.-)
GT114
GlycosylTransferases - GT114 - β-xylosyltransferase (EC 2.4.1.-)

Carbohydrate-Binding Modules
CBM1
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM1 - Modules of approx. 40 residues found almost exclusively in fungi. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in many cases, and appears to be mediated by three aromatic residues separated by about 10.4 angstrom and which form a flat surface. The only non-fungal occurence of CBM1 is in an algal non-hydrolytic polysaccharide-binding protein which is composed of four repeated CBM1 modules. Binding to chitin has been demonstrated in one case.
CBM2
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM2 - Modules of approx. 100 residues and which are found in a large number of bacterial enzymes. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in many cases. Several of these modules have been shown to also bind chitin or xylan.
CBM3
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM3 - Modules of approx. 150 residues found in bacterial enzymes. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in many cases. In one instance binding to chitin has been reported.
CBM4
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM4 - Modules of approx. 150 residues found in bacterial enzymes. Binding of these modules has been demonstrated with xylan, β-1,3-glucan, β-1,3-1,4-glucan, β-1,6-glucan and amorphous cellulose but not with crystalline cellulose.
CBM5
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM5 - Modules of approx. 60 residues found in bacterial enzymes. Chitin-binding described in several cases. Distantly related to the CBM12 family.
CBM6
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM6 - Modules of approx. 120 residues. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in one case on amorphous cellulose and β-1,4-xylan. Some of these modules also bind β-1,3-glucan, β-1,3-1,4-glucan, and β-1,4-glucan.
CBM8
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM8 - The cellulose-binding module from a cellulase of the slime mold Dictyostelium discoideum has been experimentally shown to bind cellulose.
CBM9
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM9 - Modules of approx. 170 residues found so far only in xylanases. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in one case.
CBM10
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM10 - Modules of approx. 50 residues. The cellulose-binding function has been demonstrated in one case.
CBM11
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM11 - Modules of approx. 180-200 residues. The CBM11 of Clotridium thermocellum Cel26A-Cel5E has been shown to bind both β-1,4-glucan and β-1,3-1,4-mixed linked glucans.
CBM12
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM12 - Modules of approx. 40-60 residues. The majority of these modules is found among chitinases where the function is chitin-binding. Distantly related to the CBM5 family.
CBM13
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM13 - Modules of approx. 150 residues which always appear as a threefold internal repeat. The only apparent exception to this, xylanase II of Actinomadura sp. FC7 (GenBank U08894), is in fact not completely sequenced. These modules were first identified in several plant lectins such as ricin or agglutinin of Ricinus communis which bind galactose residues. The three-dimensional structure of a plant lectin has been determined and displays a pseudo-threefold symmetry in accord with the observed sequence threefold repeat. These modules have since been found in a number of other proteins of various functions including glycoside hydrolases and glycosyltransferases. While in the plant lectins this module binds mannose, binding to xylan has been demonstrated in the Streptomyces lividans xylanase A and arabinofuranosidase B. Binding to GalNAc has been shown for the corresponding module of GalNAc transferase 4. For the other proteins, the binding specificity of these modules has not been established. The pseudo three-fold symmetry of the CBM13 module has now been confirmed in the 3-D structure of the intact, two-domain, xylanase of Streptomyces olivaceoviridis.
CBM14
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM14 - Modules of approx. 70 residues. The chitin-binding function has been demonstrated in several cases. These modules are found attached to a number of chitinase catalytic domains, but also in non-catalytic proteins either in isolation or as multiple repeats; chitin binding (EC IIa.chitin)
CBM15
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM15 - Binding to xylan and xylooligosaccharides has been demonstrated in the case of Xyn10C of Cellvibrio mixtus.
CBM16
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM16 - Carbohydrate-binding module 16. Binding to cellulose and glucomannan demonstrated [B. Bae et al (2008) J Biol Chem. 283:12415-25 (PMID: 18025086)]
CBM17
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM17 - Modules of approx. 200 residues. Binding to amorphous cellulose, cellooligosaccharides and derivatized cellulose has been demonstrated.
CBM18
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM18 - Modules of approx. 40 residues. The chitin-binding function has been demonstrated in many cases. These modules are found attached to a number of chitinase catalytic domains, but also in non-catalytic proteins either in isolation or as multiple repeats.
CBM19
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM19 - Modules of 60-70 residues with chitin-binding function.
CBM20
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM20 - The granular starch-binding function has been demonstrated in several cases. Interact strongly with cyclodextrins. Often designated as starch-binding domains (SBD).
CBM21
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM21 - Modules of approx. 100 residues. The granular starch-binding function has been demonstrated in one case. Sometimes designated as starch-binding domains (SBD).
CBM22
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM22 - A xylan binding function has been demonstrated in several cases and affinity with mixed β-1,3/β-1,4-glucans in one. In several cases a thermostabilizing effect has also been seen.
CBM23
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM23 - Mannan-binding function demonstrated in one case.
CBM24
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM24 - α-1,3-glucan (mutan)-binding function demonstrated in two cases (PMID:10636904)
CBM25
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM25 - Starch-binding function demonstrated in one case.
CBM26
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM26 - Starch-binding function demonstrated in two cases.
CBM27
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM27 - Mannan-binding function demonstrated in two cases
CBM28
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM28 - The module from the endo-1,4-glucanase of Bacillus sp. 1139 binds to non-crystalline cellulose, cellooligosaccharides, and β-(1,3)(1,4)-glucans
CBM29
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM29 - Binding to mannan/glucomannan has been demonstrated.
CBM30
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM30 - Binding to cellulose has been demonstrated for the N-terminal module of Fibrobacter succinogenes CelF.
CBM31
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM31 - Binding to β-1,3-xylan has been demonstrated for the C-terminal module of the β-1,3-xylanase of Alcaligenes sp. XY234.
CBM32
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM32 - Binding to galactose and lactose has been demonstrated for the module of Micromonospora viridifaciens sialidase (PMID: 16239725). Binding to polygalacturonic acid has been shown for a Yersinia member (PMID: 17292916). Binding to LacNAc (β-D-galactosyl-1,4-β-D-N-acetylglucosamine) has been shown for an N-acetylglucosaminidase from Clostridium perfingens (PMID: 16990278).
CBM34
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM34 - Modules of approx. 120 residues. Granular starch-binding function has been demonstrated in the case of Thermoactinomyces vulgaris R-47 α-amylase 1 (TVAI).
CBM35
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM35 - Modules of approx. 130 residues. A module that is conserved in three Cellvibrio xylan-degrading enzymes binds to xylan and the interaction is calcium dependent, while a module from a Cellvibrio mannanase binds to decorated soluble mannans and mannooligosaccharides. A module in a
CBM35inCE17
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE17 - acetylmannan esterase (3.1.1.-)
CBM36
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM36 - Modules of approx. 120-130 residues displaying structural similarities to CBM6 modules. The only CBM36 currently characterised, that from Paenbacillus polymyxa xylanase 43A, shows calcium-dependent binding of xylans and xylooligosaccharides. X-ray crystallography shows that there is a direct interaction between calcium and ligand.
CBM37
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM37 - Modules of approx. 100 residues, conserved in numerous R. albus polysaccharide-degrading enzymes and other proteins from this bacterium. Several members of CBM37 have been shown to exhibit rather broad binding specificity to xylan, chitin, microcrystalline and phosphoric-acid swollen cellulose, as well as more heterogeneous substrates, such as alfalfa cell walls, banana stem and wheat straw.
CBM38
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM38 - The inulin-binding function has been demonstrated in the case of the cycloinulo-oligosaccharide fructanotransferase from Paenibacillus macerans (Bacillus macerans) by Lee et al. (2004) FEMS Microbiol Lett 234:105-10. (PMID:15109727).
CBM39
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM39 - Modules generally found at the N-terminus of a GH16 module (itself frequently lacking a catalytic machinery) and more seldomly in isolation. The β-1,3-glucan binding function has been demonstrated, along with binding to lipopolysaccharide and lipoteichoic acid.
CBM40
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM40 - Modules of approx. 200 residues, found at the N-terminus of GH33 sialidases. Can also be found inserted in the β-propeller of GH33 sialidases. The sialic acid binding function has been demonstrated for the N-terminal CBM40 of Vibrio cholerae sialidase (Moustafa et al. (2004) J Biol Chem 279:40819-26) (PMID: 15226294).
CBM41
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM41 - Modules of approx. 100 residues found in primarily in bacterial pullulanases. The N-terminal module from Thermotoga maritima Pul13 has been shown to bind to the α-glucans amylose, amylopectin, pullulan, and oligosaccharide fragments derived from these polysaccharides (Lammerts van Bueren et al. (2004) Biochemistry 43:15633-42) (PMID: 15581376).
CBM42
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM42 - Modules of approx. 160 residues found mostly at the C-terminus of GH54 catalytic domains. Binding to arabinofuranose (present in arabinoxylan) has been demonstrated.
CBM43
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM43 - Modules of approx. 90-100 residues found at the C-terminus of GH17 or GH72 enzymatic modules and also sometimes isolated. CBM43 modules sometimes carry a C-terminal membrane anchor. The β-1,3-glucan binding function has been demonstrated with the olive pollen protein Ole e 10.
CBM44
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM44 - The C-terminal CBM44 module of the Clostridium thermocellum enzyme has been demonstrated to bind equally well cellulose and xyloglucan
CBM45
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM45 - Modules of approx. 100 residues, found at the N-terminus of plastidial α-amylases and of α-glucan, water dikinases. Starch-binding activity demonstrated in the case of potato α-glucan, water dikinase.
CBM46
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM46 - Modules of approx. 100 residues, found at the C-terminus of several GH5 cellulases. Cellulose-binding function demonstrated in one case.
CBM47
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM47 - Modules of approx 150 residues. Fucose-binding activity demonstrated
CBM48
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM48 - Modules of approx. 100 residues with glycogen-binding function, appended to GH13 modules. Also found in the beta subunit (glycogen-binding) of AMP-activated protein kinases (AMPK)
CBM49
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM49 - Modules of approx. 100 residues found at the C-terminus of plant GH9 enzymes. Distantly related to CBM2 modules. Binding to crystalline cellulose demonstrated in the case of Solanum lycopersicum Cel8 enzyme (SlCel9C1).
CBM50
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM50 - Modules of approx. 50 residues found attached to various enzymes from families GH18, GH19, GH23, GH24, GH25 and GH73, i.e. enzymes cleaving either chitin or peptidoglycan. Binding to chitopentaose demonstrated in the case of Pteris ryukyuensis chitinase A [Ohnuma T et al. (2008) J. Biol. Chem. 283:5178-87 (PMID: 18083709)]. CBM50 modules are also found in a multitude of other enzymes targeting the petidoglycan such as peptidases and amidases. These enzymes are not reported in the list below.
CBM51
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM51 - Modules of approx. 150 residues found attached to various enzymes from families GH2, GH27, GH31, GH95, GH98 and GH101 . Binding to galactose and to blood group A/B-antigens demonstrated in the case of C. perfringens GH95CBM51 and GH98CBM51 respectively [Gregg KJ et al. (2008) J. Biol. Chem. 283:12604-13 PMID: 18292090].
CBM52
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM52 - Modules of approx. 60 residues attached to a few GH81 enzymes but present in other proteins. Binding to β-1,3-glucan demonstrated for Schizosaccharomyces pombe 972h- endo-1,3-β-glucanase Eng1 [Martin-Quadrado et al., Mol. Microbiol. (2008) 69:188-200 PMID:18466295]
CBM53
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM53 - Starch-binding function demonstrated by Valdez et al. (2008) [PMID: 18260645]
CBM54
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM54 - Binding to xylan, yeast cell wall glucan and chitin shown in Dvortsov et al., Microbiology UK (2009) in press.
CBM55
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM55 - Binding to chitin demonstrated in the case of the CBM55 of Entamoeba histolytica chitinase (Van Dellen et al. (2002) Infect Immun. 70:3259 3263; PMID: 12011021).
CBM56
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM56 - β-1,3-glucan binding function demonstrated by Yamamoto et al. (1998) FEBS Letters 433:41-43 [PMID: 9738929]
CBM57
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM57 - Created from reading Schallus et al (2008) Mol Biol Cell. 19:3404-3414 [PMID: 18524852] and finding related domains attached to various glycosidases.
CBM58
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM58 - The CBM58 module of the Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron SusG protein has been shown to bind maltoheptaose
CBM59
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM59 - Binding to mannan, xylan, and cellulose demonstrated for the CBM59 of ManF-X10 xylanase from an environmental genomic DNA library (Li et al. (2009) World Journal of Microbiology and Biotechnology 25:2071-2078; doi:10.1007/s11274-009-0111-6)
CBM60
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM60 - Modules of approx 120 residues usually found appended to xylanases. The xylan-binding function and the relatedness (circular permutation) to family CBM36 has been demonstrated [PMID: 20659893].
CBM61
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM61 - Modules of approx. 150 residues found appended to GH16, GH30, GH31, GH43, GH53 and GH66 catalytic domains. A β-1,4-galactan binding function has been demonstrated for the CBM61 of Thermotoga maritima GH53 galactanase [PMID: 20826814].
CBM62
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM62 - The CBM62 module of Clostridium thermocellum Cthe_2193 protein binds galactose moieties found on xyloglucan, arabinogalactan and galactomannan.
CBM63
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM63 - The CBM63 module of Bacillus subtilis expansin EXLX1 has been shown to bind cellulose.
CBM64
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM64 - Module found at C-term of several Spirochaeta thermophila proteins. Binding to cellulose shown by Angelov, Loderer, Pompei & Liebl (2011) AEM, 77:5483-5489 [PMID - 21685171]
CBM65
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM65 - CBM65A and CBM65B, derived from Eubacterium cellulosolvens endoglucanase EcCel5A, bind to a range of β-glucans but, uniquely, display significant preference for xyloglucan
CBM66
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM66 - The CBM66 module, derived from the Bacillus subtilis exo-acting β-fructosidase SacC, targets the terminal fructoside residue of fructans.
CBM67
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM67 - Fujimoto et al. [PMID : 23486481] disclosed the L-rhamnose binding activity and 3-D structure of the CBM67 of Streptomyces avermitilis α-L-rhamnosidase (SaRha78A);
CBM68
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM68 - Binding to maltotriose and maltotetraose shown for the pullulanase of Anoxybacillus sp. LM18-11. Binding function derived from crystal structure and deletion of the CBM, which showed reduced specific activity and increased Km value compared to the wild type enzyme.
CBM69
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM69 - starch-binding function demonstrated in one case; distantly related to families CBM20 and CBM48
CBM70
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM70 - The hyaluronan-specific binding function of the N-terminal CBM70 module of Streptococcus pneumoniae hyaluronate lyase has been demonstrated.
CBM71
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM71 - The two CBM71s of S. pneumoniae BgaA bind lactose and LacNAc.
CBM72
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM72 - Modules of 130-180 residues found at the C-terminus glycoside hydrolases from various families, sometimes as tandem repeats. The CBM72 found on an endoglucanase from an uncultivated microorganism was found to bind a broad spectrum of polysaccharides including soluble and insoluble cellulose, β-1,3/1,4-mixed linked glucans, xylan, and β-mannan [PMID=26765840].
CBM73
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM73 - Modules of approx 65 residues found on various enzymes active of chitin. Chitin-binding function demonstrated for the Cellvibrio japonicus CjLPMO10A protein.
CBM74
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM74 - Modules of approx. 300 residues appended to several α-amylases. The starch-binding function has been demonstrated for the CBM74 appended to the GH13 α-amylase of Microbacterium aureum B8.A
CBM75
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM75 - Modules of 290 residues appended to GH43_16 enzymes. So far found exclusively in Ruminococci. The xyloglucan-binding function was demonstrated for the R. flavefaciens protein.
CBM76
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM76 - Modules of approx. 170 residues appended to GH44 enzymes. So far found exclusively in Ruminococci. Broad specificity binding to β-glucans demontsrated for the R. flavefaciens module, which binds xyloglucan, glucomannan, and barley β-glucan.
CBM77
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM77 - Pectin binding modules of approx. 110 residues. The Ruminococcus flavefaciens CBM77 was shown to bind various pectins of low degree of esterification.
CBM78
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM78 - Modules of approx. 150 residues appended to the C-terminus of GH5 and GH26 enzymes. So far found exclusively in Ruminococcal enzymes. The R. flavefaciens module has been shown to bind decorated β-1,4-glucans with a preference for xyloglucan.
CBM79
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM79 - Modules of approx. 130 residues found so far only in ruminococcal proteins. Binding to various β-glucans was shown for the R. flavefaciens GH9 enzyme.
CBM80
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM80 - Modules of approx. 90 residues found so far only in ruminococcal enzymes of families GH5 or GH26. Broad specificity for β-glycans (xyloglucan, glucomannan, galactomannan, barley β-glucan).
CBM81
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM81 - Small family of modules of approx. 100 residues, appended to GH5_2 enzymes. So far found exclusively in a few gammaproteobacteria. The CBM81 from an uncultivated species was shown to have binding affinity for β-1,4-, β-1,3,-glucans, xyloglucan, avicel and cellooligosaccharides.
CBM82
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM82 - The boundaries, structure and starch-binding function of the CBM82 module of Eubacterium rectale Amy13K have been reported by Cockburn and coworkers in Molec. Microbiol. (2017) (PMID=29139580)
CBM83
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM83 - The boundaries, structure and starch-binding function of the CBM83 module of Eubacterium rectale Amy13K have been reported by Cockburn and coworkers in Molec. Microbiol. (2017) (PMID=29139580)
CBM84
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM84 - Modules of approx. 140 aminoacids appended to enzymes of different families of CAZymes. A xanthan-binding function was reported for the GH9 xanthanase of Paenibacillus nanensis by Moroz et al. ACS Catal., 2018, 8 (7), pp 6021-6034.
CBM85
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM85 - Created after Fredriksen et al. (2019) PMID=30635385; binds to a broad range of glycans (cellulose, glucuronoxylan, b-1,3-1,4-glucan and glucomannan)
CBM86
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM86 - Created after the report of an activity [Leth et al. (2018), Nat. Microbiol, PMID:29610517] and a structure [Leth et al. (2019), FEBS J, PMID:31693302] of a xylan-binding module in the Xyn10A protein of Roseburia intestinalis L1-82.
CBM87
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM87 - Binding to α-1,4-N-acetylgalactosamine-rich regions of galactosaminogalactan
CBM88
Carbohydrate-Binding Modules - CBM88 - Binding to terminal galactose in galactoxyloglucan and galactomannan

Carbohydrate Esterases
CE1
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE1 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72); cinnamoyl esterase (EC 3.1.1.-); feruloyl esterase (EC 3.1.1.73); carboxylesterase (EC 3.1.1.1); S-formylglutathione hydrolase (EC 3.1.2.12); diacylglycerol O-acyltransferase (EC 2.3.1.20); trehalose 6-O-mycolyltransferase (EC 2.3.1.122)
CE2
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE2 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72).
CE3
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE3 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72).
CE4
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE4 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72); chitin deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.41); chitooligosaccharide deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.-); peptidoglycan GlcNAc deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.-); peptidoglycan N-acetylmuramic acid deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.-).
CE5
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE5 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72); cutinase (EC 3.1.1.74)
CE6
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE6 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72).
CE7
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE7 - acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72); cephalosporin-C deacetylase (EC 3.1.1.41).
CE8
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE8 - pectin methylesterase (EC 3.1.1.11).
CE9
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE9 - N-acetylglucosamine 6-phosphate deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.25); N-acetylgalactosamine 6-phosphate deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.80)
CE11
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE11 - UDP-3-0-acyl N-acetylglucosamine deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.108).
CE12
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE12 - pectin acetylesterase (EC 3.1.1.-); rhamnogalacturonan acetylesterase (EC 3.1.1.-); acetyl xylan esterase (EC 3.1.1.72)
CE13
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE13 - pectin acetylesterase (EC 3.1.1.-)
CE14
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE14 - N-acetyl-1-D-myo-inosityl-2-amino-2-deoxy-α-D-glucopyranoside deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.89); diacetylchitobiose deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.-); mycothiol S-conjugate amidase (EC 3.5.1.-)
CE15
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE15 - 4-O-methyl-glucuronoyl methylesterase (EC 3.1.1.-)
CE16
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE16 - acetylesterase (EC 3.1.1.6) active on various carbohydrate acetyl esters
CE17
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE17 - acetylmannan esterase (3.1.1.-)
CE18
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE18 - N-acetylgalactosamine deacetylase (EC 3.5.1.-)
CE19
Carbohydrate Esterases - CE19 - Family created after Bamford and coworkers [PMID=28329766] demonstrated the pectin methylesterase activity of BT1017 protein from Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron VPI-5482

Polysaccharide Lyases
PL1
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_1
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_1 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_2
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_2 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_3
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_3 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_4
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_4 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_5
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_5 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_6
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_6 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_7
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_7 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_8
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_8 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_9
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_9 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_10
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_10 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_11
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_11 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_12
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_12 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL1_13
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL1_13 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); pectin lyase (EC 4.2.2.10).
PL2
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL2 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exo-polygalacturonate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9).

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL4 - rhamnogalacturonan endolyase (EC 4.2.2.23).

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL4_1 - rhamnogalacturonan endolyase (EC 4.2.2.23).

PL5
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL5 - alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14)
PL5_1
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL5_1 - alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14)
PL6
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL6 - alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); chondroitinase B (EC 4.2.2.19); MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); poly(α-L-guluronate) lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)
PL6_1
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL6_1 - alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); chondroitinase B (EC 4.2.2.19); MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); poly(α-L-guluronate) lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)

PL6_3
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL6_3 - alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); chondroitinase B (EC 4.2.2.19); MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); poly(α-L-guluronate) lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)
PL7
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7_1 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7_2 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)
PL7_3
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7_3 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7_4 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL7_5 - poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3); α-L-guluronate lyase / G-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.11); poly-(MG)-lyase / MG-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); endo-β-1,4-glucuronan lyase (EC 4.2.2.14); oligoalginate lyase / exo-alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.26)
PL8
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL8 - hyaluronate lyase (EC 4.2.2.1); chondroitin AC lyase (EC 4.2.2.5); xanthan lyase (EC 4.2.2.12); chondroitin ABC lyase (EC 4.2.2.20); heparin lyase / heparin lyase I (EC 4.2.2.7); poly(β-mannuronate) lyase / M-specific alginate lyase (EC 4.2.2.3)

Polysaccharide Lyases - PL9 - pectate lyase (EC 4.2.2.2); exopolygalacturonate lyase (EC 4.2.2.9); thiopeptidoglycan lyase (EC 4.2.2.-); rhamnogalacturonan endolyase (EC 4.2.2.23)

PL11
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL11 - rhamnogalacturonan endolyase (EC 4.2.2.23); rhamnogalacturonan exolyase (EC 4.2.2.24)

PL12
Polysaccharide Lyases - PL12 - heparin-sulfate lyase (EC 4.2.2.8); heparin lyase / heparin lyase I (EC 4.2.2.7)

PL12_2

Auxiliary Activities
AA1
Auxiliary Activities - AA1 - Laccase / p-diphenol:oxygen oxidoreductase / ferroxidase (EC 1.10.3.2); ; ferroxidase (EC 1.10.3.-); Laccase-like multicopper oxidase (EC 1.10.3.-)
AA1_1
Auxiliary Activities - AA1_1 - Laccase / p-diphenol:oxygen oxidoreductase / ferroxidase (EC 1.10.3.2); ; ferroxidase (EC 1.10.3.-); Laccase-like multicopper oxidase (EC 1.10.3.-)

AA2
Auxiliary Activities - AA2 - manganese peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.13); versatile peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.16); lignin peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.14); peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.-); cytochrome-c peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.5); ascorbate peroxidase (EC 1.11.1.11)
AA3
Auxiliary Activities - AA3 - cellobiose dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.18); glucose 1-oxidase (EC 1.1.3.4); aryl alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.7); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); pyranose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.10)
AA3_1
Auxiliary Activities - AA3_1 - cellobiose dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.18); glucose 1-oxidase (EC 1.1.3.4); aryl alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.7); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); pyranose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.10)
AA3_2
Auxiliary Activities - AA3_2 - cellobiose dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.18); glucose 1-oxidase (EC 1.1.3.4); aryl alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.7); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); pyranose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.10)
AA3_3
Auxiliary Activities - AA3_3 - cellobiose dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.18); glucose 1-oxidase (EC 1.1.3.4); aryl alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.7); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); pyranose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.10)
AA3_4
Auxiliary Activities - AA3_4 - cellobiose dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.18); glucose 1-oxidase (EC 1.1.3.4); aryl alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.7); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); pyranose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.10)
AA4
Auxiliary Activities - AA4 - vanillyl-alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.38)
AA5
Auxiliary Activities - AA5 - Oxidase with oxygen as acceptor (EC 1.1.3.-); galactose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.9); glyoxal oxidase (EC 1.2.3.15); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); raffinose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.-)
AA5_1
Auxiliary Activities - AA5_1 - Oxidase with oxygen as acceptor (EC 1.1.3.-); galactose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.9); glyoxal oxidase (EC 1.2.3.15); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); raffinose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.-)
AA5_2
Auxiliary Activities - AA5_2 - Oxidase with oxygen as acceptor (EC 1.1.3.-); galactose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.9); glyoxal oxidase (EC 1.2.3.15); alcohol oxidase (EC 1.1.3.13); raffinose oxidase (EC 1.1.3.-)
AA6
Auxiliary Activities - AA6 - 1,4-benzoquinone reductase (EC. 1.6.5.6)
AA7
Auxiliary Activities - AA7 - glucooligosaccharide oxidase (EC 1.1.3.-); chitooligosaccharide oxidase (EC 1.1.3.-); cellooligosaccharide dehydrogenase (EC 1.1.99.-)
AA8
Auxiliary Activities - AA8 - Iron reductase domain
AA9
Auxiliary Activities - AA9 - AA9 (formerly GH61) proteins are copper-dependent lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs); cleavage of cellulose chains with oxidation of carbons C1 and/or C4 and C-6); lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C1-hydroxylating) (EC 1.14.99.54); lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C4-dehydrogenating) (EC 1.14.99.56)
AA10
Auxiliary Activities - AA10 - AA10 (formerly CBM33) proteins are copper-dependent lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs); some proteins have been shown to act on chitin, others on cellulose; lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C1-hydroxylating) (EC 1.14.99.54); lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C4-dehydrogenating)(EC 1.14.99.56); lytic chitin monooxygenase (EC 1.14.99.53); lytic xylan monooxygenase / xylan oxidase (glycosidic bond-cleaving) (EC 1.14.99.-)
AA11
Auxiliary Activities - AA11 - AA11 proteins are copper-dependent lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs); cleavage of chitin chains with oxidation of C-1 has been demonstrated for a AA11 LPMO from Aspergillus oryzae;
AA12
Auxiliary Activities - AA12 - The pyrroloquinoline quinone-dependent oxidoreductase activity was demonstrated for the CC1G_09525 protein of Coprinopsis cinerea.
AA13
Auxiliary Activities - AA13 - AA13 proteins are copper-dependent lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs); cleavage of starch with oxidation of C-1 at the site of cleavage has been demonstrated for the LPMO encoded by gene NCU08746 from Neurospora crassa; lytic starch monooxygenase / starch oxidase (glycosidic bond-cleaving) (EC 1.14.99.55)
AA14
Auxiliary Activities - AA14 - AA14 proteins are copper-dependent lytic polysaccharide monooxygenases (LPMOs); cleavage of xylan with oxidation of C-1 has been demonstrated for two AA14 LPMOs from Trametes coccinea (Pycnoporus coccineus); lytic xylan monooxygenase / xylan oxidase (glycosidic bond-cleaving) (EC 1.14.99.-)
AA15
Auxiliary Activities - AA15 - lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C1-hydroxylating) (EC 1.14.99.54); lytic chitin monooxygenase (EC 1.14.99.53)
AA16
Auxiliary Activities - AA16 - Lytic cellulose monooxygenase (C1-hydroxylating) (EC 1.14.99.54)
AA17
Auxiliary Activities - AA17 - Lytic polygalacturonic acid monooxygenase (C4-hydroxylating) (EC 1.14.99.-)
